---
title: "Barplot motif analyses hPu hPy OKseq"
output: 
date: "2026-02-20"
---

######################################################################################################
# Gustave Roussy (GR) | CNRS UMR 9019, Genome dynamics and instability Lab - Gabriel MATOS RODRIGUES 
# Developed by BEKKOUCHE Louisa - bekkouche.louisa@gmail.com                                 
# Edited by: Genome dynamics and instability Lab                                     
# v.0.1 - 2025/12/18 (for Human genome) | v.0.2 2026/02/16 (for Mouse genenome)	     
# R version 4.5.2 (2025-10-31)                                                       
# MIT licence - Copyright (c) 2025 Gabriel Matos Rodrigues			                     
# run it in a env conda for R version and enjoy your results! :)                     
#######################################################################################################

```{r}

#######################################################################################################

# Quantification of repeat motif categories across datasets
# - Loads multiple BED files (Whole genome / hPu / hPy / Pos / Neg)
# - Cleans motif strings and assigns them to defined motif groups
# - Computes motif proportions per category (Whole genome / Pos / Neg)
# - Builds a grouped barplot showing normalized percentages

#######################################################################################################

# Packages installation CRAN + BIOCONDUCTOR

cran_pkgs <- c("dplyr","ggplot2","scales", "tidyr", "stringr")

# Utility function: installs only missing packages
install_missing <- function(pkgs) {
  missing <- pkgs[!pkgs %in% rownames(installed.packages())]
  if (length(missing) > 0) {
    install.packages(missing, repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
  }
}

# Install missing CRAN packages
install_missing(cran_pkgs)

message("All required packages are installed.")

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)

#######################################################################################################

# User inputs (paths to BED files)
bed_whole_path  <- "put the whole genome bed file path"

bed_hPu_neg_path <- "put the hPu neg bed file path"
bed_hPy_neg_path <- "put the hPy neg bed file path"

bed_hPu_pos_path <- "put the hPu pos bed file path"
bed_hPy_pos_path <- "put the hPy pos bed file path"

out_plot <- "put the plot output path"

# Check that all input BED files exist
in_files <- c(
  bed_whole_path,
  bed_hPu_neg_path, bed_hPy_neg_path,
  bed_hPu_pos_path, bed_hPy_pos_path
)

stopifnot("One or more input BED files do not exist." = all(file.exists(in_files)))

# Load BED files
# BED format REQUIRED: chrom, start, end, motif (4 columns)
read_bed_strict <- function(path) {
  df <- read.table(path, sep = "\t", header = FALSE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

  # Enforce exactly 4 columns
  stopifnot(
    paste("BED file", path, "must have 4 columns.") =
      ncol(df) == 4
  )

  colnames(df) <- c("chrom", "start", "end", "motif")
  df
}

bed_whole   <- read_bed_strict(bed_whole_path)
bed_hPu_neg <- read_bed_strict(bed_hPu_neg_path)
bed_hPy_neg <- read_bed_strict(bed_hPy_neg_path)
bed_hPu_pos <- read_bed_strict(bed_hPu_pos_path)
bed_hPy_pos <- read_bed_strict(bed_hPy_pos_path)

# Standardize format: assign sample + compute repeat length
process_bed <- function(df, sample_name) {

  # Compute repeat length in bp
  df$length_bp <- df$end - df$start

  # Assign sample name
  df$sample <- sample_name

  df
}

# Apply to all datasets
bed_whole   <- process_bed(bed_whole,   "Whole genome")
bed_hPu_neg <- process_bed(bed_hPu_neg, "hPu Neg")
bed_hPy_neg <- process_bed(bed_hPy_neg, "hPy Neg")
bed_hPu_pos <- process_bed(bed_hPu_pos, "hPu Pos")
bed_hPy_pos <- process_bed(bed_hPy_pos, "hPy Pos")

# Combine datasets
bed_all <- rbind(
  bed_whole,
  bed_hPu_neg, bed_hPy_neg,
  bed_hPu_pos, bed_hPy_pos
)

# Clean motif names (remove whitespace)
bed_all <- bed_all %>%
  mutate(motif_clean = str_replace_all(motif, " ", ""))

# Define motif categories (hPu and hPy)
group1 <- c("(GA)n", "(AG)n")
group2 <- c("(GAA)n", "(AAG)n", "(AGA)n")
group3 <- c("(GAAA)n","(AAAG)n","(AAGA)n","(AGAA)n")
group4 <- c("(GGAA)n","(GAAG)n","(AAGG)n","(AGGA)n",
            "(GAAAA)n","(AAAAG)n","(AAAGA)n","(AAGAA)n","(AGAAA)n")

group5 <- c("(TC)n", "(CT)n")
group6 <- c("(TCT)n", "(TTC)n", "(CTT)n")
group7 <- c("(TTTC)n","(CTTT)n","(TCTT)n","(TTCT)n")
group8 <- c("(TTCC)n","(CCTT)n","(TCCT)n","(CTTC)n",
            "(CTTTT)n","(TTTTC)n","(TTTCT)n","(TTCTT)n","(TCTTT)n")


# Assign motif group + category (Pos / Neg / Whole genome)
bed_filtered <- bed_all %>%
  mutate(
    group = case_when(
      motif_clean %in% group1 ~ "(GA)n",
      motif_clean %in% group2 ~ "(GAA)n",
      motif_clean %in% group3 ~ "(GAAA)n",
      motif_clean %in% group4 ~ "(GAAAA)n",
      motif_clean %in% group5 ~ "(TC)n",
      motif_clean %in% group6 ~ "(TTC)n",
      motif_clean %in% group7 ~ "(TTTC)n",
      motif_clean %in% group8 ~ "(TTTTC)n",
      TRUE ~ "Other"
    ),
    category = case_when(
      sample == "Whole genome" ~ "Whole genome",
      grepl("Pos$", sample)    ~ "Pos",
      grepl("Neg$", sample)    ~ "Neg",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(category))

# Count motifs per group & category
# Complete missing combinations with n = 0
counts_df <- bed_filtered %>%
  count(group, category) %>%
  complete(group, category = c("Whole genome", "Pos", "Neg"), fill = list(n = 0))

# Normalize counts -> percentage per category
norm_df <- counts_df %>%
  group_by(category) %>%
  mutate(normalized_percentage = 100 * n / sum(n)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Plot grouped barplot
p <- ggplot(norm_df, aes(x = group, y = normalized_percentage, fill = category)) +
  geom_col(position = position_dodge(width = 0.8),
           color = "black", width = 0.8) +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Repeat percentage",
    fill = ""
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Whole genome" = "black",
    "Pos" = "springgreen4",
    "Neg" = "firebrick"
  )) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  ylim(0, 30)

print(p)

# Save plot
ggsave(
  filename = out_plot,
  plot = p,
  width = 10,
  height = 6,
  units = "in"
)
#######################################################################################################

```
